findings.desc: Redeem burns 18-decimal dreAmount but transfers the same number as 6-decimal USDC, scaling mismatch lets attacker drain all USDC reserves (or redeem reverts/underpays depending on direction).
transferFrom/transfer return values are unchecked, so a failed USDC transfer still mints dreUSD, allowing minting without backing deposit.
Donating yield via direct transfer enables a first-depositor/donation share-inflation attack that lets an attacker steal subsequent stakers' deposits.
The minter role is set but never enforced (mint is permissionless) and there is no access control, making the variable misleading and useless.

proposer.raw:
CRITICAL | dreUSD.redeem | Redeem burns 18-decimal dreAmount but transfers the same number as 6-decimal USDC, scaling mismatch lets attacker drain all USDC reserves (or redeem reverts/underpays depending on direction).
CRITICAL | dreUSD.mint | transferFrom/transfer return values are unchecked, so a failed USDC transfer still mints dreUSD, allowing minting without backing deposit.
HIGH | dreUSDs.distributeYield | Donating yield via direct transfer enables a first-depositor/donation share-inflation attack that lets an attacker steal subsequent stakers' deposits.
LOW | dreUSD | The minter role is set but never enforced (mint is permissionless) and there is no access control, making the variable misleading and useless.

results.finding: redeem() returns more than was deposited — a 6→18 decimal mismatch (redeem omits the /1e12 scale-down).
total dreUSD supply could be minted in excess of its collateral backing (insolvency).
